title: Migrate DiagnosticInfra construction to libnorn and wire into AgentBuilder
purpose: DiagnosticInfra construction — AdapterRegistry, PolicyRegistry, BypassDetector, CONVENTIONS.toml loading, and DiagnosticsPostCheck wiring — lives exclusively in norn-cli/src/runtime/wiring.rs. The AgentBuilder in the norn library crate has no way to construct or receive this infrastructure. Workflow agents built via AgentBuilder run Write/Edit with zero post-validation: no file length checks, no clippy, no bypass detection, no conventions. This brief moves the construction logic into the norn library crate so all consumers (CLI, TUI, workflow, programmatic) can build a fully-equipped agent.
task: Create a diagnostic infrastructure builder function in the norn crate that constructs DiagnosticInfra from a workspace root path and optional conventions path. Add .diagnostic_infra() and .post_checks() setter methods to AgentBuilder. Update AgentBuilder::build() to insert DiagnosticInfra as a ToolContext extension and push post_checks onto the ToolContext. Refactor norn-cli wiring.rs to call the new shared function instead of inline construction. Update make_norn_step_runner in meridian-services to call the shared function and pass results to AgentBuilder.

requirements.title: Shared DiagnosticInfra builder function in libnorn
requirements.spec: A public function build_diagnostic_infra(workspace_root: &Path) SHALL exist in a new module crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_infra.rs (NOT diagnostics_check.rs — that file is being rewritten by NTC-002 and placing new code there creates merge conflicts). It SHALL construct an AdapterRegistry (registering Rust adapters via diagnostics::languages::rust::register_rust_adapters), a PolicyRegistry (registering Rust policies via diagnostics::languages::rust::register_rust_policies), a BypassDetector (with None config), and load CONVENTIONS.toml from workspace_root.join("CONVENTIONS.toml") using ConventionsConfig::load. FileNotFound produces None conventions; other errors log a warning and produce None. The function SHALL return DiagnosticInfra with all fields populated. NOTE: this brief registers Rust adapters and policies only, matching current CLI behaviour. When TypeScript (biome), Gleam, and other language adapter registration lands, it SHALL be added to this shared function. Rust-only is the current state, not the permanent design.
acceptance: crates/norn/src/tools/diagnostics_infra.rs exists as a new module
build_diagnostic_infra exists as a public function in the new module
It constructs AdapterRegistry with Rust adapters registered
It constructs PolicyRegistry with Rust policies registered
It constructs BypassDetector with None config
It loads CONVENTIONS.toml from workspace_root, producing None on FileNotFound
It returns a fully constructed DiagnosticInfra
cargo check -p norn succeeds

requirements.title: AgentBuilder gains .diagnostic_infra() setter
requirements.spec: AgentBuilder SHALL gain a field diagnostic_infra: Option<Arc<DiagnosticInfra>> and a corresponding setter method .diagnostic_infra(infra: Arc<DiagnosticInfra>) -> Self. In build(), when diagnostic_infra is Some, the builder SHALL insert it as a ToolContext extension via ctx.insert_extension(infra) AND push a DiagnosticsPostCheck onto ctx.post_checks.
acceptance: AgentBuilder has a diagnostic_infra field of type Option<Arc<DiagnosticInfra>>
AgentBuilder has a .diagnostic_infra() setter returning Self
build() inserts DiagnosticInfra as extension when present
build() pushes DiagnosticsPostCheck onto ctx.post_checks when diagnostic_infra is present
cargo check -p norn succeeds

requirements.title: AgentBuilder gains .post_checks() setter for additional checks
requirements.spec: AgentBuilder SHALL gain a field additional_post_checks: Vec<Box<dyn RuntimePostValidateCheck>> and a setter method .post_check(check: Box<dyn RuntimePostValidateCheck>) -> Self that pushes a single check. In build(), all additional_post_checks SHALL be appended to the ToolContext post_checks vector after the diagnostic infra check (if any).
acceptance: AgentBuilder has additional_post_checks field
AgentBuilder has .post_check() setter that pushes one check
build() appends all additional_post_checks to ToolContext.post_checks
Ordering: diagnostic infra check first, then additional checks
cargo check -p norn succeeds

requirements.title: Refactor norn-cli wiring.rs to use shared function
requirements.spec: build_tool_context_with_diagnostics in norn-cli/src/runtime/wiring.rs SHALL be refactored to call norn::tools::diagnostics_check::build_diagnostic_infra (or equivalent) instead of inline-constructing AdapterRegistry, PolicyRegistry, BypassDetector, and loading CONVENTIONS.toml. The function SHALL continue to produce the same ToolContext with the same DiagnosticInfra extension and DiagnosticsPostCheck on post_checks. Behaviour is identical; the construction logic is deduplicated.
acceptance: wiring.rs no longer directly constructs AdapterRegistry, PolicyRegistry, or BypassDetector
wiring.rs no longer directly loads CONVENTIONS.toml
wiring.rs calls the shared build_diagnostic_infra function
DiagnosticInfra extension is still set on ToolContext
DiagnosticsPostCheck is still pushed onto post_checks
All existing CLI diagnostics tests continue to pass
cargo check -p norn-cli succeeds

requirements.title: Wire DiagnosticInfra into workflow caller
requirements.spec: make_norn_step_runner in crates/meridian-services/src/workflow/imperative_callbacks.rs SHALL construct a DiagnosticInfra via the shared build_diagnostic_infra function using the step working directory as workspace_root. It SHALL pass the infra to AgentBuilder via .diagnostic_infra(Arc::new(infra)). It SHALL also pass a DiagnosticCollector via the existing .diagnostics() setter. After this change, workflow agents executing Write/Edit/ApplyPatch SHALL have full post-validation (file length, clippy, bypass detection, conventions) matching CLI agents.
acceptance: make_norn_step_runner calls build_diagnostic_infra with the working directory
AgentBuilder receives .diagnostic_infra() in workflow path
AgentBuilder receives .diagnostics() in workflow path
Workflow agents running Write tool trigger DiagnosticsPostCheck
Workflow agents running Edit tool trigger DiagnosticsPostCheck
cargo check -p meridian-services succeeds

requirements.title: Re-export DiagnosticInfra and build function from norn public API
requirements.spec: DiagnosticInfra and build_diagnostic_infra SHALL be re-exported from the norn crate's public API so that external consumers (meridian-services, future programmatic users) can construct diagnostic infrastructure without reaching into internal modules. The re-export path SHALL be norn::tools::diagnostics or equivalent.
acceptance: DiagnosticInfra is accessible via norn public API
build_diagnostic_infra is accessible via norn public API
External crate can call norn::tools::diagnostics::build_diagnostic_infra without #[doc(hidden)] or crate-visibility hacks
cargo doc -p norn shows both items
